content.text:
Objectifs de l’enseignement
Nowadays, networks are increasingly large, vast, transporting large volumes of data, and developing very rapidly. These characteristics come with a great exposure to a wide variety of threats to the data carried in the networks. Today's threats require a better understanding of what data is in our networks, how we secure it, and the telltale signs left after an incident. So, it's more important now than ever to have the skills to investigate network attacks and vulnerabilities.
Contenu de la matière
Chapter 1:
Introduction to Network Forensics and Investigating Logs
Network Traffic Investigations
Chapter 2:
Web Attack Investigations
Router Forensics
Chapter 3:
Denial of Service Investigations Internet Crime Investigations Email Crime Investigations
Chapter 4:
Wireless Attack Investigations
Mobil Forensics
Chapter 6:
Corporate Espionage Investigations Trademark and Copyright Investigations Investigating Sex Related Activities
Mode d’évaluation : Contrôle continu, examen Références
• Network Forensics: Tracking Hackers through Cyberspace, Davidoff, Sherri, Ham, Jonathan
